Name: Motion Blur
Description: Applies screen-space motion blur based on camera and object velocity to simulate camera shutter exposure over time. Produces directional streaking and temporal blending to enhance motion realism or cinematic feel.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Enable Motion Blur
Fields.Description: Enables the Yebis motion blur pipeline, applying velocity-based blur as part of post-processing.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Blur Time Ratio
Fields.Description: Controls how motion blur accumulates over time relative to frame duration, affecting streak persistence.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Number of Base Samples
Fields.Description: Defines the base sample count used along motion vectors for blur reconstruction.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Max Blur Length
Fields.Description: Limits the maximum length of motion blur streaks to prevent excessive smearing.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Motion Blur Scale
Fields.Description: Scales the contribution of motion vectors to blur intensity, acting as a multiplier for motion magnitude.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Max Recurrences
Fields.Description: Controls the number of recursive blur passes used to accumulate motion blur for stronger trailing effects.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Sample Interleaved
Fields.Description: Enables interleaved sampling patterns to reduce banding and improve performance during blur sampling.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Camera Rotation Perspective Threshold
Fields.Description: Threshold controlling how camera rotation contributes to motion blur intensity.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Camera Translation Threshold
Fields.Description: Threshold controlling how camera movement contributes to motion blur intensity.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Falloff Distance Minimum
Fields.Description: Minimum depth at which motion blur begins to attenuate based on distance from the camera.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Falloff Distance Maximum
Fields.Description: Maximum depth at which motion blur fully fades out, reducing distant blur artifacts.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Enable Bidirectional Permission
Fields.Description: Allows motion blur to be applied in both forward and backward directions along motion vectors.
